title: symphytum davisii subsp. naxicola
intro: Symphytum davisii subsp. naxicola is a plant species within the Symphytum genus, commonly known as comfrey, a perennial herb with edible leaves and roots that have been used for medicinal purposes for centuries. The plant's medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits such as wound healing, reducing inflammation and pain, and improving bone health. Comfrey is traditionally used in herbal preparations like teas, infusions, and tinctures to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems, as well as to promote tissue repair and bone growth. Its leaves and roots can be consumed raw or cooked, and its herbal preparations can be applied topically or taken internally to harness its medicinal properties.
